headline: Specifications are not substitutes for executable logic
lede: The push toward agentic coding assumes that natural language specifications can bypass the rigor of traditional software development. Reality suggests that when documentation becomes this granular, it functions as code by another name, complete with all the vulnerabilities of uncompiled logic.

headline: Orchestrating Claude workflows with Cook
lede: Cook introduces a structured approach to AI interaction by chaining tasks through automated operators. It moves beyond simple interactive prompts, allowing developers to implement logic loops and review gates directly into their coding pipeline.

headline: Thousands of interviews reveal the human cost of AI
lede: A qualitative study of 81,000 global users uncovers deep-seated tensions regarding the future of work. As AI integration accelerates, the primary concern remains the friction between productivity gains and the potential for cognitive atrophy.

headline: Nvidia GreenBoost offloads VRAM to system memory
lede: This Linux kernel module attempts to extend GPU VRAM capacity by utilizing system RAM and NVMe storage via DMA-BUF. While it allows larger models to run on modest hardware, the latency introduced by PCIe bandwidth remains a formidable obstacle.

headline: How Austin’s aggressive density policies lowered local rents
lede: By systematically reforming zoning laws and eliminating parking requirements, Austin successfully increased its housing stock by 120,000 units. The resulting surplus offers a rare empirical look at how supply-side interventions can counteract regional rent inflation.

headline: The precarious life of a software mechanic
lede: In a future dominated by natural language generation, the role of the developer has shifted to that of a repairman. We watch as systems grow increasingly opaque, creating a fragile landscape where one minor model update can collapse an entire farm's infrastructure.

headline: Designing flight vehicles with OpenRocket
lede: This open-source simulator brings professional-grade aerodynamic analysis to the hobbyist level. By tracking stability metrics and center-of-gravity variables, engineers can model complex flight paths with high fidelity before the first stage even ignites.

headline: Autonomous agents iterate on MaxSAT solvers
lede: The agent-sat project utilizes Claude to refine heuristic solvers for complex MaxSAT problems without human oversight. By sharing strategies across a repository, these agents aim to bridge the gap between static algorithms and adaptive, self-improving search techniques.

headline: Rob Pike’s 1989 rules for maintainable code
lede: Thirty-five years later, Pike’s principles remain a vital rebuke to premature optimization and gratuitous complexity. The most efficient systems are rarely the most clever; they are the most transparent.
pullquote: Data structures are the primary architecture, while code logic remains secondary to the clear organization of information.

headline: The stagnation of the lean startup methodology
lede: Despite decades of design thinking and customer development, startup survival rates remain stubbornly flat. It appears that when methodology becomes standardized, it ceases to function as a competitive advantage and instead becomes a baseline, yielding no further growth.
